name: Taipei (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: youth, peace, and the green hills around the city
purity, brightness, and the integrity of the city's people

description: A green field charged at the centre with a white stylised flower emblem composed of five petals around a central core marking the kanji 北 (běi, 'north').
history: Taipei, Taiwan's capital and largest special municipality, adopted the current city flag on 28 March 1981. The five-petal emblem stylises the plum blossom — the national flower of the Republic of China — and incorporates the character 北 ('north') from 臺北 (Taipei, 'Northern Terrace') as the central motif. The plum blossom's five petals are traditionally said to represent the five branches of government and the Three Principles of the People.

features.symbolism: youth, peace, and the green hills surrounding Taipei

features.role: Taipei City emblem — plum blossom with the kanji 北
features.symbolism: a five-petalled plum blossom, the national flower of the Republic of China, with the character 北 ('north') at its centre, representing Taipei (臺北, 'Northern Terrace') and the city's heritage as the capital of the Republic
